_comment: Real-PDF corpus for the boilerplate-detection benchmark (scripts/benchmark_boilerplate.py --real). Real documents have no ground-truth labels, so each entry records (a) the public source for reproducibility, and (b) `known_boilerplate`: regexes for boilerplate confirmed by manual inspection of the margin bands, which the detector MUST catch (a recall check). PDFs themselves are gitignored under benchmark_data/.real_pdfs/ and re-fetched from `source`.

documents.title: Attention Is All You Need
documents.notes: NIPS-2017 preprint format. Page numbers bottom-center; no running header. Page-1-only notices (Google attribution, NIPS footer) are NOT boilerplate and must be left in place.

documents.title: Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR)

known_boilerplate.pattern: official journal of the european union
known_boilerplate.desc: running header on every page; 'L 119/N' page-ref increments

documents.notes: Official Journal format. The bottom-of-page footnotes ('(1) OJ C 229, ...') vary per page and must NOT be stripped.

documents.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners (GPT-3)
documents.notes: Page numbers bottom-center; no running header. Page-1-only author notices must be left in place.
